balanced.comment: ACTIVE default: Claude reasoning seams + Gemini throughput. researcher (and the extract_facts step it feeds) run on gemini-2.5-flash -- the benchmark 'throughput king' for high-volume source processing -- with claude-opus-4-6 as the quality backup. supervisor + final_report stay on opus (dispatch + synthesis quality); haiku for bulk summarize/compress. Cross-backend backups throughout. Revert to all-Claude with MODEL_ROUTING_PRESET=claude. NB the gemini-primary roles need the gemini CLI logged in (see .env.example).

gemini.comment: Gemini-primary, Claude cross-backend backup. Each role keeps the verified gemini-2.5-flash primary; on quota/404/auth it fails over to the benchmark's per-role Claude winner (only opus reliably dispatches as supervisor). Claude backups need only the subscription; no extra setup. extract_facts is overridden to gemini-2.5-PRO: flash only satisfies the broad whole-profile ExtractionResult schema ~2/4 of the time (then fails over to opus), whereas pro is 4/4 -- pro primary keeps extraction on gemini (no Claude burn); opus-4-6 stays as backup.

claude.comment: Claude-primary (benchmark per-role mix: only opus dispatches as supervisor; haiku for bulk summarize/compress), gemini-2.5-flash cross-backend backup if Claude throttles. The flash backup needs the gemini CLI logged in (see .env.example); without it the backup call just fails too -- no worse than no backup.

codex.comment: ChatGPT/Codex CLI primary with gemini then claude backups. NB codex exec is agentic (runs repo commands); keep its backend sandbox read-only before using in anger (see .env.example). Two backups because codex is the least proven backend here.

fast.comment: Lowest-latency preset: Gemini throughput on the hot paths (researcher + per-page summarize/compress run gemini-2.5-flash, the verified fast/cheap throughput king with 1M context). Supervisor uses claude-opus-4-8 to DISPATCH research -- repo benchmark: only opus reliably dispatches, and the supervisor is low-volume (a few planning calls) so opus here costs little wall-clock while avoiding the worst runtime outcome: an empty-notes run you have to redo. Cross-backend gemini-2.5-flash supervisor backup also dispatches (proven live). final_report on claude-sonnet-4-6 (fast, coherent synthesis). Bulk roles fail over cross-backend to claude-haiku-4-5. Activate with MODEL_ROUTING_PRESET=fast. Gemini-primary roles need the gemini CLI logged in (see .env.example), else every primary call fails over to Claude.

nvidia.comment: EXAMPLE preset for NVIDIA's OpenAI-compatible API (integrate.api.nvidia.com). Needs NVIDIA_API_KEY set (key-authenticated, independent of Claude subscription mode); the 'nvidia:' prefix routes to a ChatOpenAI pointed at that endpoint. Tool-calling/structured-output support varies by NVIDIA-hosted model, so every role keeps a cross-backend Claude backup that takes over on any failure. The NIM 'thinking' knobs are auto-applied per model (ON for nemotron*, OFF for others like nvidia:minimaxai/minimax-m3); override with NVIDIA_ENABLE_THINKING / NVIDIA_REASONING_BUDGET / NVIDIA_TEMPERATURE / NVIDIA_TOP_P (see .env.example). Swap the nemotron id for whatever NVIDIA model you have access to. Activate with MODEL_ROUTING_PRESET=nvidia.

agy.comment: Antigravity CLI (agy) backend -- the working replacement after the gemini CLI free tier was deprecated. Dispatch (supervisor) routed to Claude-via-agy for reliable tool-calling; gemini-3.x throughput for research/text; claude_agent_sdk backups everywhere (independent of agy auth). extract_facts on gemini-3.5-flash-high (the lean schema makes flash sufficient).
